mission: Recover the repo-visible source of truth for the lost PPLUS Metabase reporting structure.

generated_from.role: independent earlier proof of workload and muscle reporting views
repo-visible Metabase restore automation showing base URL, database id, and root collection naming
repo-visible evidence that PPLUS Reporting was used as the restore collection
strongest repo-visible consolidation of missing workload and fatigue collection, question, and dashboard target names

confidence_summary.notes: SQL is definitive for the reporting layer. Metabase content names are partly recoverable from repo-visible Conductor-chain artifacts, but no serialized dashboard export or full application metadata dump was found in the repo.

reporting_schema.purpose: Read-optimized reporting layer for Metabase and internal analytics dashboards.

reporting_views.name: reporting.completed_session_summary
reporting_views.purpose: Primary high-level reporting source for completed workout sessions.

reporting_views.name: reporting.discarded_session_summary
reporting_views.purpose: Reporting source for discarded or abandoned sessions.

reporting_views.name: reporting.exercise_completion_history
reporting_views.purpose: Completed exercise history with best-set rollup.

reporting_views.name: reporting.exercise_performance_history
reporting_views.purpose: Derived exercise performance snapshots for progress reporting.

reporting_views.name: reporting.pr_events
reporting_views.purpose: First-pass PR events derived from strength snapshots.

reporting_views.name: reporting.muscle_load_event_history
reporting_views.purpose: Raw completed-set muscle load drilldown source.

reporting_views.name: reporting.muscle_fatigue_summary
reporting_views.purpose: Session-by-session muscle load rollup for fatigue reporting.

reporting_views.name: reporting.athlete_adherence_summary
reporting_views.purpose: Assigned versus completed or discarded workout summary per athlete.

reporting_views.name: reporting.coach_activity_summary
reporting_views.purpose: Coach-level rollup of athletes, assignments, and outcomes.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Reporting - Workload & Fatigue
dashboards.layout_guidance: Place workload trend and fatigue summary above the raw event-history drilldown.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Workflow Monitor v2
dashboards.layout_guidance: Operational dashboard from prior repo-visible restore evidence, retained as supporting context rather than the primary lost workload/fatigue target.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Session Anomalies
dashboards.layout_guidance: Operational anomaly dashboard from prior repo-visible restore evidence, retained as supporting context rather than the primary lost workload/fatigue target.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Reporting - Sessions
dashboards.layout_guidance: Overview cards first, exception and discard detail below.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Reporting - Performance & PRs
dashboards.layout_guidance: Lead with PR and performance summaries, keep detailed history lower on the page.

dashboards.name: PPLUS Reporting - Adherence & Coach Ops
dashboards.layout_guidance: Pair athlete adherence with coach activity in a two-column layout.

connection_assumptions.preferred_connectivity: Use Supabase Session pooler credentials in Metabase unless direct connection is specifically required.
must_preserve: The reporting layer should remain read-only for Metabase consumption.
The schema and views are designed to be rerunnable with create or replace semantics.
Completed-session filtering is a core reporting assumption for most workload and muscle views.

recoverability_notes: No repo-visible Metabase export or dashboard serialization was recovered.
Exact dashcard coordinates, visualization settings, and filters are not provable from the repo.

The safest rebuild path is: restore SQL exactly, preserve PPLUS Reporting as root, restore workload/fatigue names exactly where evidence is explicit, and map the remaining views into straightforward one-view-per-question content.
